ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for Crystal Nut Bliss specifically; FOODON_03301710 (nut product) is the nearest defensible parent category.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP (nut-based confectionery) used as closest parent; no exact code exists for this proprietary Malaysian nut cake product.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT code identified for this specific nut confection product.

serving.natural_language_portion: One typical serving of Crystal Nut Bliss is approximately 80 g, equivalent to one slice or individually portioned piece as served at a Malaysian café or coffee bar.

nutrient_source.title: Mixed nut confection / nut bar — composite estimate derived from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for mixed nuts, honey, butter, and egg white; cross-referenced with IMR Malaysia MyFCD nut-based snack category values.
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service (FoodData Central SR Legacy) and Institute for Medical Research Malaysia (IMR MyFCD)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Crystal Nut Bliss is a proprietary product sold at EC Coffee Bar, Carpenter Street, Singapore. No exact entry exists in any approved primary database. Nutrient values are estimated from a composite of USDA SR Legacy entries for mixed nuts (dry roasted, USDA FDC NDB 12137), honey (USDA FDC NDB 19296), butter (USDA FDC NDB 01001), and egg white (USDA FDC NDB 01124), weighted to reflect a typical nut-dominant confection (approximately 55% nuts, 20% sweetener, 15% binding agent, 10% fat). IMR Malaysia MyFCD nut snack category used for cross-validation. Values represent a best-estimate per 100 g of the finished product.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of nut-based confections and mixed nut bars — analogous estimate based on mixed nuts (GI ~15) and honey/sugar-bound nut bars (GI ~40–55) from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. (2008) International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Crystal Nut Bliss. GI estimated at 45 based on analogous nut-based confections in the University of Sydney GI Database: mixed nuts have GI ~15; honey-bound nut bars and similar nut confections range GI 40–55. A midpoint estimate of 45 is used, reflecting the dominant nut content (which suppresses GI) offset by the sugar/honey binder. Glycemic load per serving = (45 × 42.0 / 100) × 0.80 kg serving = (45 × 33.6 g carb per serving) / 100 = 15.1. Calculation: GI 45 × carbohydrate per serving 33.6 g / 100 = 15.1 GL units.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium content (~55 mg per 100 g); potassium and magnesium from nuts support vascular health
hypertension.guidance: Crystal Nut Bliss is low in sodium (~44 mg per 80 g serving) and provides potassium and magnesium from mixed nuts, making it a relatively hypertension-friendly treat when consumed in moderate portions; however, total caloric density should be monitored in individuals managing weight-related hypertension.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load (~15 per serving) driven by sugar/honey binder; nut content attenuates glycemic response
type2_diabetes.guidance: With an estimated GI of 45 and a glycemic load of approximately 15 per 80 g serving, Crystal Nut Bliss has a moderate glycemic impact; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit portion size to half a serving and pair with a low-GI meal to avoid postprandial glucose spikes driven by the sugar and honey binder content (~28 g sugar per 100 g).

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High total fat (34 g/100 g) predominantly unsaturated; saturated fat moderate at 6.5 g/100 g; caloric density high at ~498 kcal/100 g
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The predominantly unsaturated fat profile from mixed nuts (monounsaturated and polyunsaturated fats ~24.5 g/100 g) is cardioprotective, but the high caloric density and moderate saturated fat content (6.5 g/100 g) mean that individuals with cardiovascular disease should restrict intake to small portions (40–50 g) and account for this item within their daily saturated fat budget of less than 20 g.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Crystal Nut Bliss is a Malaysian-origin nut-based confection sold at EC Coffee Bar on Carpenter Street, Singapore. It is a dense, sweet cake or bar made primarily from a blend of mixed nuts — typically cashews, almonds, walnuts, and peanuts — bound together with honey or sugar syrup and a small quantity of butter or oil and egg white, then baked or set until firm. The texture is crunchy and chewy simultaneously, with a rich, toasty nut flavour balanced by the sweetness of the honey or sugar glaze. Culturally, nut confections of this style are popular across Malaysian and Singaporean café culture, often served as a premium snack or dessert alongside coffee or tea. Nutritionally, Crystal Nut Bliss is energy-dense at approximately 498 kcal per 100 g, with a high fat content (34 g/100 g) that is predominantly heart-healthy unsaturated fats from the nuts. Protein content is moderate at 10.5 g/100 g. Carbohydrate content is 42 g/100 g with 28 g sugar, reflecting the sweetener used in binding. Dietary fibre is 3.5 g/100 g. The glycemic index is estimated at 45 (low GI), as the high nut content attenuates the glycemic response despite the sugar binder. Clinically, it is a moderate-concern food for Type 2 Diabetes due to its sugar content and glycemic load (~15 per serving), and a moderate-concern food for cardiovascular disease due to caloric density, though the unsaturated fat profile is favourable. Sodium is low (~55 mg/100 g), making it suitable for hypertension management in small portions.

confidence_reason: Crystal Nut Bliss is a proprietary product from a single restaurant (EC Coffee Bar, Carpenter Street, Singapore) with no entry in any approved primary nutritional database (HPB Singapore Foodo, IMR Malaysia MyFCD, USDA FoodData Central, or FAO/INFOODS SEA). Nutrient values are estimated from a composite of USDA SR Legacy entries for the likely component ingredients. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for similar nut-based confections. Confidence is therefore low.
notes: 1. Crystal Nut Bliss is a proprietary café product; no exact nutritional data exists in any approved primary source. All per_100g values are composite estimates from USDA SR Legacy ingredient data and IMR Malaysia MyFCD nut snack category cross-validation. These values should be replaced with laboratory-verified data if available. 2. vitamin_d_mcg is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy entries for the component ingredients and is not expected to be a significant contributor in a nut-based confection without fortification. 3. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are all estimated from USDA SR Legacy component data and are approximate. 4. GI of 45 is an analogous estimate; gi_category is low (GI ≤ 55 per Veritas threshold rules). Glycemic load per serving = GI × carbohydrate per serving (g) / 100 = 45 × 33.6 / 100 = 15.1. 5. Dietary flags: is_vegan set to false due to likely egg white and butter content; is_halal set to true as the product is sold at a Malaysian coffee bar and no pork or alcohol ingredients are indicated, but this should be verified against the restaurant's halal certification. 6. The food_name 'Crystal Nut Bliss' is preserved from the field survey source record as the canonical English name; no alternative canonical name was found in approved sources. 7. Multilingual local names are descriptive translations/transliterations for a Malaysian-cuisine item sold in Singapore; they are not verified official product names in those languages.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Built full RESULT JSON from scratch; composite nutrient estimate constructed from USDA SR Legacy and IMR Malaysia MyFCD; GI analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008.

Iteration 2: Verified aliases_by_language consistency against flat aliases list; confirmed all strings match verbatim. Verified GI category threshold (GI 45 ≤ 55 = low). Confirmed vitamin_d_mcg -1 sentinel documented in notes. Confirmed no zero core macros. Confirmed ontology match_type values are only 'exact', 'closest_parent', or 'none'. Confirmed natural_language_description exceeds 200 characters. All hard gates and Q1–Q8 verified as passing.
